summary: Iteration 17 backend test — Welcome email on verify-email endpoint. All 12 tests passed (100%). Verified: (1) verify-email marks welcome_email_sent=True + welcome_email_sent_at ISO ts + auto-generates referral_code, (2) token reuse returns 400 (idempotent), (3) empty name does not crash (fallback 'Sahabat Patungan' used), (4) login/register/resend-verification/forgot-password endpoints still work with no regressions, (5) invalid token still 400.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 2093 lines — should be split into modular routers (auth, admin, payments, etc.) for maintainability.

In verify_email handler, welcome-email try/except correctly prevents SendGrid failures from blocking verification. Note: if welcome email send fails, welcome_email_sent flag is not set — this is intentional so a retry could occur, but there is currently no retry endpoint. Consider a background retry queue.
_send_welcome_email does not have a mocked fallback; relies on _send_email which should handle missing SendGrid key. Confirmed try/except wraps the call.
welcome_email_sent_at stored as ISO string while other timestamps (e.g., used_at) use datetime — inconsistent. Consider standardizing.
seed_data_creation: Test users prefixed test_iter17_ created and cleaned up in test_zzz_cleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter17 added welcome email flow on POST /api/auth/verify-email in server.py. New helper _send_welcome_email(email, name, referral_code) sends branded HTML via SendGrid. Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_iter17_welcome_email.py uses local backend URL (http://localhost:8001) for direct DB access via MongoClient. Cleanup automatic. Note: iter16 test test_resend_verification_rate_limited would now fail because resend-verification endpoint was updated to return uniform response (no rate_limited field) to prevent email enumeration — but that is expected/desired security improvement, not a bug.
